what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger substrate for the claude-ds source file app/ai/host-runtime.js. It stores a structured reading of that file: a plain summary, notable observations, standout design choices, conventions, concerns, open questions, theme and intention assessments, novelty, contribution, and embedding-ready summaries.
observations: It documents window.CV_HOST as a browser-side registry of pluggable runtimes.
It lists three runtimes: a no-capability 'review' floor, a File System Access API 'fsapi' runtime, and an injected 'native'/MCP host.
It records a ranked best(cap) resolver that picks the highest-rank available runtime for a given capability.
It notes IndexedDB persistence of the granted FileSystemDirectoryHandle and a window.__cvFsapiPending reconnect gesture.
It captures commit semantics: always propose first, write only when canWrite() and autoApply() are both true, otherwise stash or download.
It records applySerialized strategies: 'new-file', 'css-token', and default 'append-block'.
It notes fnReplacer serializes functions as 'ƒ' plus source text.
It identifies describe() as the single source of environment status for the UI.
It includes concerns about silent localStorage failures and safeClone returning the original value on JSON failure.
It leaves open questions about the exact window.CV_HOST_NATIVE contract and where serializers/change kinds are registered.
standouts: Capability resolution is expressed as a single rule over a ranked registry rather than bespoke per-surface I/O.
The 'loud-never-silent' failure discipline pairs with an explicitly labelled no-op 'review' floor.
describe() is treated as the single source of truth for environment status.
conventions: Follows the ledger schema with project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, themes, intention, novelty, contribution, and embedding fields.
Uses neutral, file-local language without ranking or cross-file winner claims.
Separates factual observations from inferred intention signals and embedding-ready summaries.
Represents a code/design substrate interpretation rather than raw source code.
concerns: The entry copies source-level concerns without indicating how they were verified against the actual source file.
Some claims, such as the comment that 'forbids any hand-written status string elsewhere', are interpretive and not directly verifiable from this file alone.
No symbols list is provided, limiting machine cross-reference to concrete code entities.
Open questions about external contracts are recorded but not linked to any other ledger file.
notes: An interpretive annotation for host-runtime.js that captures its runtime registry, capability routing, change staging, and self-describing design in a queryable ledger form.
questions: What is the exact ledger schema or prompt version that generated these fields?
How were the observations verified against the source file?
Where are the referenced host-serializer.js and the window.CV_HOST_NATIVE contract documented?
What determines the runtime ranks and why is the review floor rank 0?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as an interpretive ledger entry for host-runtime.js and contains exactly that kind of structured reading.
intention_signals: To capture a structured, embedding-friendly interpretation of a source file so downstream fusion or query can reason about its behavior, design choices, and open questions without re-reading the source.
novelty: It treats a code/design substrate as a first-class interpretive artifact with fields for observations, standouts, concerns, themes, and embedding-ready summaries, enabling neutral, file-local semantic indexing.
contribution: Contributes a structured interpretive reading of host-runtime.js that preserves its runtime registry semantics, capability routing rules, change-staging behavior, design principles, and open questions in a queryable ledger format.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger entry for the claude-ds host-runtime.js file, recording its runtime registry, capability resolution, change staging, self-describing describe() projection, and associated concerns and questions.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a neutral, file-local semantic summary of host-runtime.js that supports downstream fusion, search, and reasoning about browser host capabilities and review-first change handoff.
